jokes.text: But there's cab fare on the nightstand. / That's the third morning in a row you've done that. / We're married.
jokes.setup: Dennis treating his new wife like a one-night stand by leaving cab fare
jokes.punchline: Maureen pointing out they're married and she's already home
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates Dennis's self-centered nature and inability to comprehend normal relationships. The setup feels natural and the punchline lands hard.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: But there's cab fare on the nightstand.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: That's the third morning in a row you've done that.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: We're married.

jokes.text: Oh, I don't work. / Oh, oh, okay. / I just thought maybe you weren't going to work recently because we were going through a little bit of a honeymoon phase. But you're saying that you don't have a job. / No job. / No job at all? Wow.
jokes.setup: Dennis realizes his new wife doesn't work
jokes.punchline: His dawning horror at the reality of his situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's superficial understanding of marriage, but fairly predictable development.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: Oh, I don't work.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, oh, okay. I just thought maybe you weren't going to work recently because we were going through a little bit of a honeymoon phase. But you're saying that you don't have a job.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: No job.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No job at all? Wow.

jokes.text: All yours. / All mine. / All mine all the time. / All the time.
jokes.setup: Maureen enthusiastically claiming ownership of Dennis
jokes.punchline: The increasingly uncomfortable repetition and possessiveness
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation through repetition, building the uncomfortable dynamic between them.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: All yours.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: All mine.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: All mine all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: All the time.

jokes.text: Don't-don't look in those drawers. / What's in those drawers? / My sex tapes.
jokes.setup: Dennis warning Maureen not to snoop
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he keeps sex tapes in a drawer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - narcissistic, creepy, and delivered with complete casualness. Great economy and character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Don't-don't look in those drawers.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: What's in those drawers?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: My sex tapes.

jokes.text: You told me it was the phone bill, man. / Of course I signed it. / Why would you think that you needed to sign a phone bill.
jokes.setup: Charlie complaining about signing what he thought was a phone bill
jokes.punchline: Mac's logical point about why you wouldn't sign a phone bill
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie logic - he's so confused about normal life he'd sign a phone bill. Mac's response highlights the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You told me it was the phone bill, man. / Of course I signed it.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Why would you think that you needed to sign a phone bill.

jokes.text: When he hands me something to sign and it's about money and papers, I sign it.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his financial decision-making process
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of understanding of legal documents
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie - demonstrates his childlike approach to adult responsibilities. Very character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: When he hands me something to sign and it's about money and papers, I sign it.

jokes.text: Charlie, gets health insurance and I get diddly-squat.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining the benefits of their fake marriage
jokes.punchline: Mac getting nothing while Charlie gets practical benefits
jokes.explanation: Functional joke about the imbalanced relationship, but not particularly memorable or clever.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie, gets health insurance and I get diddly-squat.

jokes.text: Because I don't have a home anymore. So this is where I take a shower. This is where I live, Dennis.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking why Mac is bathing in the bar sink
jokes.punchline: Mac revealing he's homeless and living at the bar
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag premise and establishes Mac's desperate situation. The delivery sells the pathetic reality.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because I don't have a home anymore. So this is where I take a shower. This is where I live, Dennis.

jokes.text: Yeah, one second you're on your high horse about how great marriage is, and now you're destroying one? / Well, I'm not the one who's married. I didn't do anything wrong.
jokes.setup: Mac calling out Dee's hypocrisy about marriage
jokes.punchline: Dee's weak justification that she's not technically married
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Dee's self-serving logic, but fairly standard hypocrisy joke.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, one second you're on your high horse about how great marriage is, and now you're destroying one?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not the one who's married. I didn't do anything wrong.

jokes.text: Guys, guys, guys, guys, guys, all this talk about marriage and Dee being such a whore has got me thinking... I never got a bachelor party.
jokes.setup: Dennis building up to a serious realization
jokes.punchline: His shallow concern is about missing a bachelor party
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and very Dennis-like priorities. The casual 'whore' comment adds to the character authenticity.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Guys, guys, guys, guys, guys, all this talk about marriage and Dee being such a whore has got me thinking... I never got a bachelor party.

jokes.text: Being married to Charlie's got me all confused. I got to get my hetero on.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining why he needs to go to a strip club
jokes.punchline: His concern about appearing heterosexual due to fake gay marriage
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific humor about Mac's sexuality insecurities and the absurd situation they've created.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Being married to Charlie's got me all confused. I got to get my hetero on.

jokes.text: I like that move. What is that? / That's it. Motorcycle? / That's pretty... Yeah, not like that, though. It's pretty good, but you got to use... a little bit more shoulder.
jokes.setup: The guys practicing strip club dance moves
jokes.punchline: Dennis seriously critiquing Charlie's fake motorcycle dancing technique
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy setup with Dennis taking something ridiculous way too seriously, which is in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I like that move. What is that?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That's it. Motorcycle?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's pretty... Yeah, not like that, though. It's pretty good, but you got to use... a little bit more shoulder.

jokes.text: Now do I write the word 'too' or should I write the number two? What's the best way to do that when we're texting back and forth?
jokes.setup: Dennis texting Maureen back
jokes.punchline: His neurotic overthinking of basic texting grammar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis neurosis - overthinking trivial details while missing bigger picture. Very character-specific behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Now do I write the word 'too' or should I write the number two? What's the best way to do that when we're texting back and forth?

jokes.text: Sorry. You have to be a couple for that. / We are a couple. / Charlie, documents.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie wanting a couple's dance
jokes.punchline: Mac treating their fake marriage like legitimate documentation
jokes.explanation: 'Documents' delivery is perfect - Mac treating absurd situation with bureaucratic seriousness. Great callback execution.

dialogue.speaker: Stripper
dialogue.line: Sorry. You have to be a couple for that.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We are a couple.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: documents.

jokes.text: I'm tightening the purse strings on this relationship until there's a little bit of give and take.
jokes.setup: Mac refusing to give Charlie money at the strip club
jokes.punchline: Mac using relationship power dynamics language for their fake marriage
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about Mac taking their fake marriage seriously. The language is appropriately ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm tightening the purse strings on this relationship until there's a little bit of give and take.

jokes.text: 'Miss you more.' That's classic Maureen right there. She's always got to one-up me 'Miss you most.' Most... can't get more than most, right? So that should... that should be the end of it, right?
jokes.setup: Dennis analyzing a text exchange with Maureen
jokes.punchline: His competitive approach to expressing affection and false confidence about 'winning'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis characterization - turning love into competition and overthinking it. The logic is perfectly flawed.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: 'Miss you more.' That's classic Maureen right there. She's always got to one-up me 'Miss you most.' Most... can't get more than most, right? So that should... that should be the end of it, right?

jokes.text: Actually, I came clean to my wife. And she kicked me out of the house. / Ew. Ouch. / Yeah. Bet you wish you could take that one back.
jokes.setup: Bill revealing he confessed to his wife
jokes.punchline: Dee's completely unsympathetic and casual response to his life falling apart
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee's selfishness and lack of empathy. 'Ew. Ouch.' is perfectly dismissive.

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: Actually, I came clean to my wife. And she kicked me out of the house.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Ew. Ouch.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah. Bet you wish you could take that one back.

jokes.text: Dee, I gave you a car. / You're gonna jam the car in my face, huh?
jokes.setup: Bill reminding Dee about the car gift
jokes.punchline: Dee's accusatory response to his reasonable point
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing how Dee turns even reasonable requests into personal attacks on her.

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: Dee, I gave you a car.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You're gonna jam the car in my face, huh?

jokes.text: That is not your room anymore. It is my craft studio, so kindly stay out of there.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to go to his room
jokes.punchline: Maureen has already converted it to a craft studio
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Maureen taking over Dennis's life. The craft studio detail is perfectly absurd and specific.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: That is not your room anymore. It is my craft studio, so kindly stay out of there.

jokes.text: She turned it into her craft studio where she makes terrible sweatshirts out of cats or... puts cats in sweatshirts
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining Maureen's craft studio
jokes.punchline: The bizarre and unclear nature of her cat-based crafts
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd detail. The uncertainty about whether cats are materials or subjects makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: She turned it into her craft studio where she makes terrible sweatshirts out of cats or... puts cats in sweatshirts

jokes.text: I don't love you, Maureen. I don't love you. I never loved you. You're annoying and you're strange.
jokes.setup: Dennis drunkenly confronting Maureen
jokes.punchline: His brutal and repetitive honesty while drunk
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment showing Dennis's cruelty and emotional immaturity. The repetition emphasizes his drunken state.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I don't love you, Maureen. I don't love you. I never loved you. You're annoying and you're strange.

jokes.text: It's like her breath... Ugh! It's like always so bad. It's like a dead tooth. It's the dead tooth. I'm saying it now. I'll say it. You have a dead tooth.
jokes.setup: Dennis continuing his drunken rant
jokes.punchline: His fixation on Maureen's dead tooth and building courage to say it
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character work. Dennis's shallow vanity and cruelty perfectly captured. The build-up to saying it makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's like her breath... Ugh! It's like always so bad. It's like a dead tooth. It's the dead tooth. I'm saying it now. I'll say it. You have a dead tooth.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? How about if you guys don't leave, I call the cops and I tell 'em you beat me?
jokes.setup: Standoff between Dennis and Maureen
jokes.punchline: Maureen threatening to falsely accuse Dennis of domestic violence
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that shows how terrible both characters are. Effective shock value while staying in character.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah? How about if you guys don't leave, I call the cops and I tell 'em you beat me?

jokes.text: Oh, shit, that's from... that's from Fear, dude. Oh, that's Marky Mark. That's Marky Mark. That's what Marky Mark does.
jokes.setup: Maureen punching herself
jokes.punchline: Mac recognizing it as a specific movie reference and getting excited about it
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but relies on recognition humor. Mac's enthusiasm for movie trivia in a crisis is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, shit, that's from... that's from Fear, dude. Oh, that's Marky Mark. That's Marky Mark. That's what Marky Mark does.

jokes.text: Sorry. Want to have sex? / What?! No, no! God, no! Please close your legs up.
jokes.setup: Bill casually asking Dee for sex while she's trying to help him
jokes.punchline: Dee's disgusted reaction and request for him to close his legs
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with visual comedy implied. The casual proposition and disgusted response work well together.

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: Sorry. Want to have sex?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What?! No, no! God, no! Please close your legs up.

jokes.text: I don't give a shit about the car, okay? / Mm-hmm. / My life is in ruins. / Mm-hmm. / I'm gonna lose my kids. / Okay, great, so we're in agreement. I keep the car.
jokes.setup: Bill explaining his life is falling apart
jokes.punchline: Dee completely missing the point and focusing only on keeping the car
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Dee's complete selfishness. Perfect misdirection and timing on the punchline.

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: I don't give a shit about the car, okay? My life is in ruins. I'm gonna lose my kids.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm. Mm-hmm. Okay, great, so we're in agreement. I keep the car.

jokes.text: I don't give a shit about the car, okay? My life is in ruins. I'm gonna lose my kids. Okay, great, so we're in agreement. I keep the car.
jokes.setup: Bill expressing genuine despair
jokes.punchline: Dee only hearing what she wants about the car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee selfishness - completely ignoring human suffering to focus on material gain. The misdirection is expertly crafted.

dialogue.speaker: Bill
dialogue.line: I don't give a shit about the car, okay? My life is in ruins. I'm gonna lose my kids.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Okay, great, so we're in agreement. I keep the car.

jokes.text: You think it's gonna be that easy, dude? / Well, yeah, of course. I mean, marriage and divorce these days, it's like a, it's more of a formality than anything else, you know?

jokes.punchline: His casual dismissal of marriage as just a formality
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis characterization showing his arrogance and complete misunderstanding of consequences.

dialogue.line: You think it's gonna be that easy, dude?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, yeah, of course. I mean, marriage and divorce these days, it's like a, it's more of a formality than anything else, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Nobody washes the dishes! We eat the food directly off the coffee table, and you know it!

jokes.punchline: Their only art is Charlie's elephant drawings, treated seriously as assets
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor about their poverty and Charlie's childlike artistic efforts being treated as legitimate assets.

dialogue.line: All I'm seeing is a series of drawings of elephants.

dialogue.line: Oh, yeah. Well, Charlie did the elephant drawings.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, but... You should keep 'em.

jokes.text: If I didn't know any better, I'd say there's a lot of love in this room here.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Mac being unexpectedly sweet during their divorce
jokes.punchline: Jack's observation about love during a divorce proceeding
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation about the absurdity of their affectionate divorce. Clean and well-delivered.

dialogue.line: If I didn't know any better, I'd say there's a lot of love in this room here.

jokes.text: Why did we have to do all the touching and the hand-holding if it was just...? / It was fun.
jokes.setup: Mac questioning their earlier physical affection
jokes.punchline: Charlie's simple, honest response that he enjoyed it

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Why did we have to do all the touching and the hand-holding if it was just...?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It was fun.

jokes.text: I just kidnapped Bill Ponderosa's kids.
jokes.setup: Dee calling someone for help
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to kidnapping as her opening line
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation. The casual delivery of something so serious is expertly done and very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I just kidnapped Bill Ponderosa's kids.

jokes.text: I just kidnapped Bill Ponderosa's kids.

jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Perfect cold open to a scene - matter-of-fact delivery of shocking information. Economy and shock value are excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I just kidnapped Bill Ponderosa's kids.

jokes.text: All right, are the kids confined in any way, maybe in a crawl space or a backyard bunker? / No, they're out in the car. / Have you touched them yet? / Absolutely not.

jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact criminal consultation
jokes.explanation: Excellently dark humor with the caller treating kidnapping like a normal consultation. The specific questions are perfectly absurd.

dialogue.line: All right, are the kids confined in any way, maybe in a crawl space or a backyard bunker?

Have you touched them yet?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Absolutely not.

jokes.punchline: Her attempt to deflect responsibility for kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee's complete lack of accountability, even for kidnapping. Very much in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I tell you what, I did not mean to kidnap them. That was all... that was all this guy.

Yes.
Okay.
